title: Galápagos Islands 3-Day Wildlife Safari
metaTitle: Galápagos Islands 3-Day Wildlife Safari | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Galápagos Islands, Ecuador: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Galápagos National Park entry fee and Charles Darwin Research Station. Prices from $1131 per person (estimate).
highlights: Multi-day live-aboard dive or snorkel cruise
Premium small-ship expedition cruise with a naturalist guide
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Galápagos Islands, Ecuador — Two seasons: a warm, wetter period December-May (highs around 29-30°C) and a cooler, drier garúa season June-November (highs around 24-26°C, with misty mornings). June to November for cooler, drier weather and the best marine wildlife viewing (nutrient-rich currents); December to May is warmer with better underwater visibility for snorkeling. This itinerary blends Galápagos National Park entry fee, Charles Darwin Research Station, Tortuga Bay with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Galápagos National Park entry fee, Charles Darwin Research Station, Tortuga Bay
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Galápagos Islands
itinerary.description: Land at Seymour Airport (Baltra) (GPS) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Galápagos National Park entry fee — Mandatory park fee covering most land visitor sites. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Galápagos Islands: Charles Darwin Research Station & Multi-day live-aboard cruise
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Charles Darwin Research Station — Giant tortoise breeding center on Santa Cruz Island. In the afternoon, continue to Multi-day live-aboard cruise — The classic way to see multiple islands and open-ocean wildlife. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Snorkeling excursion at Kicker Rock or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to GPS for your departure flight.

travelTips: Decide between a multi-day live-aboard cruise (sees more islands, pricier) and a land-based stay with day tours (cheaper, more flexible) before booking.
Book the National Park-mandated naturalist guide as part of any tour — independent access to most sites isn't allowed.
Bring cash (USD) for smaller purchases — card acceptance is inconsistent outside larger hotels.
Book flights and park permits well in advance during the July-August and December-January peak weeks.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Reef-safe sunscreen (regular sunscreen is restricted/discouraged to protect marine life)
Sturdy water shoes for lava-rock landings
Snorkel gear or a rash guard
Light rain jacket for the garúa season
thingsToDo: Multi-day live-aboard dive or snorkel cruise
Scuba diving with hammerhead sharks (advanced divers)
Charles Darwin Research Station tortoise viewing
thingsToAvoid: Touching or feeding any wildlife, or straying off marked trails — fines are strictly enforced.
Bringing non-reef-safe sunscreen or single-use plastics onto the islands.
Underestimating inter-island travel time when planning a multi-island land-based itinerary.
topRestaurants: Isla Grill — Grilled seafood and meats ($$)
Kioskos de Puerto Ayora — Street-side seafood grills ($)
instagramSpots: Marine iguanas on black lava rock
Sea lions on Puerto Ayora docks
Giant tortoises at the Darwin Station
hiddenGems: Los Gemelos twin sinkholes, Santa Cruz highlands
Concha de Perla snorkeling cove, Isabela Island
familyActivities: Charles Darwin Research Station tortoise viewing
adventureActivities: Multi-day live-aboard dive or snorkel cruise
Scuba diving with hammerhead sharks (advanced divers)
luxuryExperiences: Premium small-ship expedition cruise with a naturalist guide
Luxury eco-lodge stay on Santa Cruz
honeymoonExperiences: Private snorkel excursion with sea turtles
Secluded beach picnic on a day-tour island
budgetExperiences: Puerto Ayora fish market and waterfront
Land-based budget tours instead of a live-aboard cruise
localEtiquette: Strict conservation rules apply — never touch, feed, or approach wildlife closer than the mandated distance (usually 2m).
Stay on marked trails at all times within the National Park.
Licensed naturalist guides are mandatory for most land visitor sites — always travel with one.
Take all trash with you; nothing should be left on the islands.
localTransportGuide: Inter-island travel is by small plane or public speedboat ferry (around 2 hours between main islands). Within Puerto Ayora, everything is walkable; a mandatory licensed guide accompanies almost all excursions to National Park visitor sites, either via day tours or a live-aboard cruise.
bestTimeToVisit: June to November for cooler, drier weather and the best marine wildlife viewing (nutrient-rich currents); December to May is warmer with better underwater visibility for snorkeling.
weather: Two seasons: a warm, wetter period December-May (highs around 29-30°C) and a cooler, drier garúa season June-November (highs around 24-26°C, with misty mornings).
visaInformation: Ecuador is visa-free for most nationalities (including US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, India) for stays up to 90 days. All visitors must pay a Galápagos National Park entry fee ($100-200 depending on nationality) and obtain a Transit Control Card on arrival.
averageFlightTime: ~17.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Galápagos Islands?
faqs.answer: Ecuador is visa-free for most nationalities (including US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia, India) for stays up to 90 days. All visitors must pay a Galápagos National Park entry fee ($100-200 depending on nationality) and obtain a Transit Control Card on arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Galápagos Islands?
faqs.answer: June to November for cooler, drier weather and the best marine wildlife viewing (nutrient-rich currents); December to May is warmer with better underwater visibility for snorkeling. Two seasons: a warm, wetter period December-May (highs around 29-30°C) and a cooler, drier garúa season June-November (highs around 24-26°C, with misty mornings).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Galápagos Islands safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Galápagos Islands is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Galápagos Islands?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Lucas Sharma
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Puerto Ayora waterfront and Playa de los Alemanes. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Omar Novak
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Multi-day live-aboard cruise and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Andersen
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Our trip to Galápagos Islands was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Multi-day live-aboard cruise was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Emma Garcia
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Galápagos Islands was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Charles Darwin Research Station was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Nadia Rossi
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Galápagos Islands is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Puerto Ayora waterfront. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Wei Williams
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Playa de los Alemanes in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Chen Garcia
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Snorkeling excursion at Kicker Rock in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Andersen
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Snorkeling excursion at Kicker Rock alone was worth the trip, but combined with Puerto Ayora waterfront this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.
